description: RED metrics (Rate, Errors, Duration) for all Java microservices, derived from OTel HTTP server metrics and trace span-metrics. Covers per-service and per-route HTTP traffic, service-to-service dependencies, JVM summary, and Kubernetes infra (paid tier only).

options.content:
## CI-CD / Microservices Overview — read me first

Pick the tier in **`deployment_environment`** (stable | develop) and narrow with **`service_name`**.

| | **stable** | **develop** |
|---|---|---|
| Source | `main` branch → `microservices` ns | `develop` branch → `microservices-develop` ns |
| Topology | full (HA DBs) | lean (single-instance DBs) |

### Signals on this board
- **HTTP RED** (`http_server_*`) — **R**ate, **E**rrors (4xx/5xx), **D**uration (p50/p95/p99), per service & route.
- **Span-metrics RED** (`traces_spanmetrics_*`) — RED derived from traces, per service / operation / span-kind.
- **Service dependency graph** (`traces_service_graph_*`) — service→service edges (rate / failed / latency). ⚠️ This metric has **no `deployment_environment` label**, so its panels show **all tiers combined**.
- **JVM & runtime** — heap / GC / threads / CPU summary; full depth in the **CI-CD JVM internals** dashboard.

### Dashboard sections at a glance

| Section | What it answers |
|---|---|
| **Overview** | What are the headline throughput, error, and latency numbers right now? |
| **HTTP traffic** | Which services and routes are busy, slow, or erroring? |
| **Span metrics (OTel traces)** | What does trace-derived RED look like per service and span kind? |
| **Service dependency graph** | Which service-to-service edges have failures or high latency? |
| **JVM summary** | Is memory or CPU pressure emerging across the fleet? |
| **Kubernetes pod & container infra** | Are pods restarting? Is capacity sufficient? (empty only on Grafana Cloud free tier) |
| **Logs & traces** | Per-request drill-down via logs and distributed traces. |

### Why a panel may show no data
- 🟢 **`0` = healthy** — error rates and GC pauses sit at 0 when nothing is wrong or the service is idle.
- 📈 **Needs traffic** — every RED panel is rate-based; an idle service draws a flat 0 line. Generate load with the **`Day2.traffic.01-k6`** workflow.
- 🌐 **Service graph = all tiers** — `traces_service_graph_*` has no `deployment_environment` label, so that section is **not** filtered by the tier picker.

### ⚠️ Grafana Cloud free tier only — the *Kubernetes pod & container infra* section is empty there (on OSS/Azure/AWS the collector self-scrapes cAdvisor, so it populates)
`config.yaml → observability.grafanaCloudTier` defaults to **`free`**, which enables **`leanMetrics`**: the collector drops cluster-infra metrics (kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor: `kube_pod_*`, `kube_deployment_*`, `container_cpu_*`, `container_memory_*`) to stay under the free-tier 15k active-series cap. Everything else — `http_server_*`, `traces_*`, `jvm_*` — is always shipped. To enable the infra section, set `grafanaCloudTier: paid` and re-run `Day1.cluster.01-gke`.

panels.description: Distinct services emitting HTTP metrics.
panels.title: Services reporting

panels.description: Distinct pods currently emitting HTTP server metrics — a proxy for pods actively handling traffic.
panels.title: Active pods

panels.description: Total HTTP requests per second across all selected services.
panels.title: Request rate

panels.description: Percentage of requests returning 5xx server errors. Any sustained non-zero value warrants investigation.
panels.title: Error rate (5xx)

panels.description: Percentage of requests returning 4xx client errors (bad request, not found, unauthorized, etc.).
panels.title: Client errors (4xx)

panels.description: Percentage of requests returning 2xx responses — the complement of the 4xx and 5xx error rates.
panels.title: Success (2xx)

panels.description: Median HTTP request duration across all selected services.
panels.title: Latency p50

panels.description: 95th-percentile request duration — the primary SLO reference latency.
panels.title: Latency p95

panels.description: 99th-percentile request duration. If p99 ≫ p95, there are outlier requests worth investigating.
panels.title: Latency p99

panels.description: Mean request duration across all selected services. Use alongside percentiles to understand the latency distribution shape.
panels.title: Avg latency

panels.description: Server-side span throughput from trace data — a secondary throughput signal independent of HTTP metrics.
panels.title: Span calls/s (server)

panels.description: Fraction of spans marked as errors in trace data. Correlate with the HTTP 5xx rate to find non-HTTP error paths.
panels.title: Span error rate

panels.description: HTTP requests per second broken down per service. Shows which service is handling the most traffic.
panels.title: Request rate by service

panels.description: Request rate split by HTTP response status code across all services. Lets you spot 4xx/5xx spikes fleet-wide.
panels.title: Requests by status code

panels.description: Latency distribution over time across all selected services. Spikes indicate transient slowdowns.
panels.title: HTTP latency p50 / p95 / p99

panels.description: Combined client + server error rate per service over time. Sustained non-zero values require investigation.

targets.expr: 100 * (sum by (service_name) (rate(http_server_request_duration_seconds_count{deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", service_name=~"$service_name", http_response_status_code=~"[45].."}[$__rate_interval])) or (sum by (service_name) (rate(http_server_request_duration_seconds_count{deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", service_name=~"$service_name"}[$__rate_interval])) * 0)) / clamp_min(sum by (service_name) (rate(http_server_request_duration_seconds_count{deployment_environment="$deployment_environment", service_name=~"$service_name"}[$__rate_interval])),1)

panels.title: Error rate by service (4xx+5xx)

panels.description: Top 10 routes by request volume — identifies high-traffic endpoints for capacity planning and SLO targeting.
panels.title: Request rate by route (top 10)

panels.description: Top routes by p95 latency — stable snapshot, sorted descending
panels.title: Latency p95 by route (top 10)

panels.description: Server-side span throughput per service from trace data. Complements HTTP metrics and covers non-HTTP call paths.
panels.title: Span calls/s by service (server)

panels.description: Rate of error spans per service. Rising values indicate failures not always visible in HTTP status codes.
panels.title: Span error calls/s by service

panels.description: 95th-percentile span duration per service from trace data — includes processing time for non-HTTP work.
panels.title: Span duration p95 by service

panels.description: Span throughput by kind (server / client / internal / producer / consumer). Reveals async and messaging traffic patterns.
panels.title: Calls/s by span kind
Service dependency graph (all tiers — no deployment_environment label)

panels.description: ⚠️ Not filtered by environment or service — traces_service_graph_* metrics do not carry deployment_environment or service_name labels in this setup. Shows all edges across all environments.
panels.title: Edge request rate (client → server)

panels.description: ⚠️ Not filtered by environment or service — traces_service_graph_* metrics do not carry deployment_environment or service_name labels in this setup. Shows all edges across all environments.
panels.title: Edge failed requests/s

panels.description: ⚠️ Not filtered by environment or service — traces_service_graph_* metrics do not carry deployment_environment or service_name labels in this setup. Shows all edges across all environments.
panels.title: Edge latency p95 — server side

panels.description: ⚠️ Not filtered by environment or service — traces_service_graph_* metrics do not carry deployment_environment or service_name labels in this setup. Shows all edges across all environments.
panels.title: Edge latency p95 — client side
JVM & runtime (summary — deep dive in 'CI-CD JVM internals')

panels.description: JVM heap per service. Monotonic growth indicates memory pressure. See JVM internals dashboard for depth.
panels.title: Heap used by service

panels.description: 99th-percentile GC pause per service. Spikes above ~200 ms correlate with latency spikes in HTTP panels.
panels.title: GC pause p99 by service

panels.description: Total live JVM threads per service. Steady growth may indicate a thread leak.
panels.title: Live threads by service

panels.description: Recent JVM CPU utilization per service (0–1). Sustained values above 0.8 indicate overload.
panels.title: JVM CPU utilization by service

panels.description: Total JVM memory (heap + non-heap) per service. High non-heap growth suggests metaspace or code-cache accumulation.
panels.title: JVM memory used by service (heap + non-heap)

panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor. Populated on OSS, Azure and AWS (the collector self-scrapes these) and on Grafana Cloud paid; empty only on Grafana Cloud free tier, where leanMetrics drops these series (set grafanaCloudTier=paid).
panels.title: Pod restarts (rate)

panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor. Populated on OSS, Azure and AWS (the collector self-scrapes these) and on Grafana Cloud paid; empty only on Grafana Cloud free tier, where leanMetrics drops these series (set grafanaCloudTier=paid).
panels.title: Container memory (working set)

panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor. Populated on OSS, Azure and AWS (the collector self-scrapes these) and on Grafana Cloud paid; empty only on Grafana Cloud free tier, where leanMetrics drops these series (set grafanaCloudTier=paid).
panels.title: Container CPU (cores)

panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor. Populated on OSS, Azure and AWS (the collector self-scrapes these) and on Grafana Cloud paid; empty only on Grafana Cloud free tier, where leanMetrics drops these series (set grafanaCloudTier=paid).
panels.title: Deployment replicas (desired vs available)

panels.description: Requires kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor. Populated on OSS, Azure and AWS (the collector self-scrapes these) and on Grafana Cloud paid; empty only on Grafana Cloud free tier, where leanMetrics drops these series (set grafanaCloudTier=paid).
panels.title: Pods by phase

panels.description: Filter by $level. trace_id in a line links to the trace (logs→traces via derived fields).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
traces
| where '${operation_id}' == '' or operation_Id == '${operation_id}'
| project timestamp, severityLevel, cloud_RoleName, operation_Id, message
| order by timestamp desc

panels.title: Microservices pod logs — $deployment_environment

panels.description: Click a Trace ID → trace view (links to logs & metrics via the Tempo datasource config).

azureLogAnalytics.query:
union requests, dependencies
| where '${operation_id}' == '' or operation_Id == '${operation_id}'
| project timestamp, operation_Id, cloud_RoleName, name, duration, success, itemType
| order by timestamp desc

panels.title: Recent traces — $service_name

azureResourceGraph.query: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.insights/components' | project id | order by id asc

list.description: Filter the logs panel by severity (detected_level). All includes plain-text (unknown).
list.label: Log level
list.name: level

title: CI-CD / Microservices Overview
